fields.title: Local development workflow and fixture data
fields.content:
## Local development refresh

We refreshed the recommended workflow for contributors running **archweb** on their
own machines. The steps below are split across several short paragraphs so you
can see how line breaks render on the home page teaser and on the full article.

First, sync dependencies and apply migrations in a clean tree.

Second, load the bundled fixtures so package search, mirrors, and related pages
have something to render without pulling live data.

Typical commands:

    uv sync
    uv run ./manage.py migrate
    uv run ./manage.py loaddata main/fixtures/*.json

If you only need a subset, adjust the glob. When something goes wrong, check the
traceback and the Django logs before opening an issue.

Shell helpers (same idea, different shell features):

    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=settings
    uv run ./manage.py check

That is all for this announcement.

fields.title: [core] linux rebuild (no ABI change)
fields.content:
### linux package rebuild

A routine rebuild landed in [core] with no ABI changes expected.

Highlights from the maintainer notes:

    # pacman -Syu
    # reboot if you replaced the running kernel

Nothing else is required unless you use out-of-tree modules.

fields.title: Wiki mirror rotation completed
fields.content:
Wiki mirrors were rotated. No user action is needed.

Example session you might run locally (one indented code block, multiple lines):

    # Step 1: response headers
    curl -I https://wiki.archlinux.org/
    # Step 2: status code only (body discarded)
    curl -sL -o /dev/null -w 'status=%{http_code}\n' https://wiki.archlinux.org/
    # Step 3: quick timing summary
    curl -sL -o /dev/null -w 'namelookup=%{time_namelookup}s connect=%{time_connect}s total=%{time_total}s\n' \
        https://wiki.archlinux.org/
    echo 'Connectivity check complete'

The response should be **HTTP/2** or **HTTP/1.1** with a normal status code.

fields.title: Illustrative maintenance schedule
fields.content:
Upcoming schedule (all dates are illustrative for this fixture):

- Monday: sync databases
- Tuesday: run the full test suite

Inline check:

    pytest -q

End of list demo.

fields.title: Python snippet for preview formatting
Plain text line breaks in one paragraph
fields.content:
Plain paragraph fixture with soft line breaks in the source
file: this sentence continues after a single newline inside the paragraph in
Markdown, which usually joins lines into one HTML paragraph when you view the
full article.
